name: dnsrecon
title: dnsrecon
description:
Powerful DNS enumeration script
DNSRecon is a Python script that provides the ability to perform:
.
* Check all NS Records for Zone Transfers.
* Enumerate General DNS Records for a given Domain
(MX, SOA, NS, A, AAAA, SPF and TXT).
* Perform common SRV Record Enumeration.
* Top Level Domain (TLD) Expansion.
* Check for Wildcard Resolution.
* Brute Force subdomain and host A and AAAA records
given a domain and a wordlist.
* Perform a PTR Record lookup for a given IP Range or CIDR.
* Check a DNS Server Cached records for A, AAAA and CNAME
* Records provided a list of host records in a text file to check.
* Enumerate Hosts and Subdomains using Google

command_examples: dnsrecon [-h] [-d DOMAIN] [-iL INPUT_LIST] [-n NS_SERVER] [-r RANGE] [-D DICTIONARY] [-f] [-a]
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dnsrecon because Powerful DNS enumeration script.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dnsrecon as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dnsrecon [-h] [-d DOMAIN] [-iL INPUT_LIST] [-n NS_SERVER] [-r RANGE] [-D DICTIONARY] [-f] [-a]` as documented upstream for dnsrecon.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dnsrecon -t axfr -d zonetransfer.me` as documented upstream for dnsrecon.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dnsrecon with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dnsrecon --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install dnsrecon. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `dnsrecon -t axfr -d zonetransfer.me`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Powerful DNS enumeration script.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: dnsrecon - DNS Enumeration and Scanning Tool.
